title: FFPE RNA Library Preparation with NEBNext Kits
author: New England Biolabs
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.tumenu6
materials: NEBNext Ultra II Directional RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina

steps.title: Add 3 µL of the above...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL of the above mix to 12 µL total RNA (from Step 1), resulting in a total volume of 15 µL

steps.title: Mix by pipetting up and down at least 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting up and down at least 10 times.

steps.title: Spin down briefly in a microcentrifuge
steps.instruction: Spin down briefly in a microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Place samples in a thermocycler with...
steps.instruction: Place samples in a thermocycler with a heated lid set to approximately 105 °C , and run the following program, which will take approximately 15–20 minutes to complete: %s %s Temp Time 95°C 2 min 95-22°C 0.1°C/sec 22°C 5 min hold

steps.title: Spin down the samples in a...
steps.instruction: Spin down the samples in a microcentrifuge, and place on ice. Proceed immediately to RNase H Digestion Step.

steps.title: Assemble the RNAse H master mix on ice as follows
steps.instruction: Assemble the RNAse H master mix on ice as follows. %s %s Component Volume NEBNext RNase H 2 μl RNase H Reaction Buffer 2 μl Nuclease-free Water 1 μl Total Volume 5 μl NEBNext RNase H New England Biolabs Catalog #E6318 RNase H Reaction Buffer New England Biolabs Catalog #E6312 Nuclease-free Water New England Biolabs Catalog #E6317

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times.

steps.title: Briefly spin down the samples in a microcentrifuge
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the samples in a microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Add 3 µL of the RNase...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL of the RNase H master mix to the RNA sample from Step 7, resulting in a total volume of 20 µL

steps.title: Incubate the sample in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in a thermocycler for 00:30:00 at 37 °C with the lid set to 40 °C (or on).

steps.title: Briefly spin down the samples in...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the samples in a microcentrifuge, and place on ice. Proceed immediately to DNase I Digestion to prevent non-specific degradation of RNA.

steps.title: Assemble the DNase I master mix...
steps.instruction: Assemble the DNase I master mix on ice in a nuclease-free tube. %s %s DNase I Master Mix Volume DNase I Reaction Buffer 5 μl DNase I (RNase-free) 2.5μl Nuclease-free Water 22.5μl Total Volume 30 μl DNase I Reaction Buffer New England Biolabs Catalog #E6315 DNase I (RNase-free) New England Biolabs Catalog #E6316 Nuclease-free Water New England Biolabs Catalog #E6317

steps.title: Briefly spin down the sample in a microcentrifuge
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the sample in a microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Add 30 µL of DNase I...
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL of DNase I master mix to 20 µL RNA sample from Step 14, resulting in a total volume of 50 µL

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate the sample in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in a thermocycler for 00:30:00 at 37 °C with the heated lid set to 40 °C (or on).

steps.title: Briefly spin down the sample in...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the sample in a microcentrifuge, and place on ice. Proceed immediately to RNA Purification.

steps.title: Vortex Agencourt RNAClean XP Beads or...
steps.instruction: Vortex Agencourt RNAClean XP Beads or RNA Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 110 µL (2
steps.instruction: Add 110 µL (2.2X) beads to the RNA sample from Step 21 and mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. NEBNext Sample Purification Beads New England Biolabs Catalog #E6315

steps.title: Incubate the sample for 00:15:00 on...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample for 00:15:00 on ice to bind RNA to the beads.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack to separate beads from the supernatant. After the solution is clear, carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads, which contain RNA.

steps.title: Repeat Step 26 once for a...
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 26 once for a total of 2 washing steps. go to step #26

steps.title: Completely remove residual ethanol, and air...
steps.instruction: Completely remove residual ethanol, and air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with the lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of RNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet. Elute the RNA from the beads by adding 7 µL Nuclease-free Water. Mix well by pipetting up and down at least 10 times and briefly spin the tube. Nuclease-free Water New England Biolabs Catalog #E6317

steps.title: Incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature. Place the tube in the magnet until the solution is clear (~ 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Remove 5 µL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Remove 5 µL of the supernatant containing RNA and transfer to a nuclease-free tube.

steps.title: Place the sample on ice and...
steps.instruction: Place the sample on ice and proceed to Priming of Highly Degraded RNA.

steps.title: Assemble the following fragmentation and priming...
steps.instruction: Assemble the following fragmentation and priming reaction on ice : %s %s Fragmentation and Priming Reaction Volume Ribosomal RNA Depleted Sample (Step 32) 5 µl NEBNext First Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer 4 µl Random Primers 1 µl Total Volume 10 µl Random Primers New England Biolabs Catalog #E7422

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times.

steps.title: Prepare a RNA/Probe master mix as follows
steps.instruction: Prepare a RNA/Probe master mix as follows: %s %s Component Volume NEBNext rRNA Depletion Solution 1 μl Probe Hybridization Buffer 2 μl Total Volume 3 μl NEBNext rRNA Depletion Solution New England Biolabs Catalog #E6313 NEBNext Probe Hybridization Buffer New England Biolabs Catalog #E6314

steps.title: Dilute the total RNA with Nuclease-free...
steps.instruction: Dilute the total RNA with Nuclease-free Water to a final volume of 12 µL in a PCR tube. Keep the RNA on ice.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down at least 10 times.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack. Incubate at room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain the RNA. 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) Contributed by users

steps.title: Incubate the sample in a preheated thermocycler as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in a preheated thermocycler as follows: 00:05:00 at 65 °C , with heated lid set at 105 °C Hold at 4 °C

steps.title: Transfer the tube directly to ice...
steps.instruction: Transfer the tube directly to ice and proceed to First Strand cDNA Synthesis.

steps.title: Assemble the first strand synthesis reaction...
steps.instruction: Assemble the first strand synthesis reaction on ice by adding the following components to the fragmented and primed RNA from Step 37: %s %s First Strand Synthesis Reaction Volume Fragmented and Primed RNA (Step 36) 10 µl NEBNext Strand Specificity Reagent 8 µl NEBNext First Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix 2 µl Total Volume 20 µl NEBNext Strand Specificity Reagent New England Biolabs Catalog #E7766 NEBNext First Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7761

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times.

[ ! ] Incubate the sample in a preheated thermocycler with the heated lid set at ≥ 80 °C as follows: Note: If you are following recommendations in Appendix A (Chapter 6), for libraries with longer inserts (> 200 bases), increase the incubation at 42°C from 15 minutes to 50 minutes at Step 2 below. Step 1: 00:10:00 at 25 °C Step 2: 00:15:00 at 42 °C Step 3: 00:15:00 at 70 °C Step 4: Hold at 4 °C

steps.title: Proceed directly to Second Strand cDNA Synthesis
steps.instruction: Proceed directly to Second Strand cDNA Synthesis.

steps.title: Assemble the second strand cDNA synthesis...
steps.instruction: Assemble the second strand cDNA synthesis reaction on ice by adding the following components into the first strand synthesis product from Step 40. %s %s Second Strand Synthesis Reaction Volume First Strand Synthesis Product (Step 40) 20 µl NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer with dUTP (10X) 8 µl NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix 4 µl Nuclease-free Water 48 µl Total Volume 80 µl NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer with dUTP Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7426 NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7425 Nuclease-free Water New England Biolabs Catalog #E7764

steps.title: Keeping the tube on ice, mix...
steps.instruction: Keeping the tube on ice, mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down at least 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate in a thermocycler for 01:00:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a thermocycler for 01:00:00 at 16 °C with the heated lid set at ≤ 40 °C (or off).

steps.title: Vortex SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend. NEBNext Sample Purification Beads New England Biolabs Catalog #E7767

steps.title: Add 144 µL (1
steps.instruction: Add 144 µL (1.8X) of resuspended beads to the second strand synthesis reaction (~ 80 µL ). Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Briefly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge to collect any sample on the sides of the tube. Place the tube on a magnet to separate beads from the supernatant. After the solution is clear, carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads, which contain DNA. Caution: Do not discard beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack. Incubate at room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Fresh 80% Ethanol Contributed by users

steps.title: Repeat Step 49 once for a...
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 49 once for a total of 2 washing steps. go to step #49

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack. Elute the DNA from the beads by adding 53 µL 0.1X TE Buffer (provided) to the beads. Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Quickly spin the tube and incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature. Place the tube on the magnetic rack until the solution is clear. (0.1X) TE Buffer New England Biolabs Catalog #E7763

steps.title: Remove 50 µL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Remove 50 µL of the supernatant and transfer to a clean nuclease-free PCR tube. If you need to stop at this point in the protocol samples can be stored at –20°C.

steps.title: Assemble the end prep reaction on...
steps.instruction: Assemble the end prep reaction on ice by adding the following components to the second strand synthesis product from Step 53. %s %s End Prep Reaction Volume Second Strand Synthesis Product (Step 52) 50 µl NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer 7 µl NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 3 µl Total Volume 60 µl If a master mix is made, add 10 µL of master mix to 50 µL of cDNA for the End Prep reaction. NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer New England Biolabs Catalog #E7647 NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7646

steps.title: Set a 100 μl or 200...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μl or 200 μl pipette to 50 μl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. It is important to mix well. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance.

steps.title: Incubate the sample in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in a thermocycler with the heated lid set at ≥ 75 °C as follows. 00:30:00 at 20 °C 00:30:00 at 65 °C Hold at 4 °C

steps.title: Proceed immediately to Adaptor Ligation
steps.instruction: Proceed immediately to Adaptor Ligation.

[ ! ] Dilute the NEBNext Adaptor* prior to setting up the ligation reaction in ice-cold Adaptor Dilution Buffer and keep the adaptor on ice. %s %s Total RNA Input Dilution Required 1,000 ng–101 ng 5–fold dilution in Adaptor Dilution Buffer 100 ng–10 ng 25–fold dilution in Adaptor Dilution Buffer 5 ng 200–fold dilution in Adaptor Dilution Buffer *The NEBNext adaptor is provided in NEBNext oligos kit. NEB has several oligo kit options, which are supplied separately from the library prep kit.

steps.title: Assemble the ligation reaction on ice...
steps.instruction: Assemble the ligation reaction on ice by adding the following components, in the order given, to the end prep reaction product from Step 5. %s %s Ligation Reaction Volume End Prepped DNA (Step 56) 60 µl Diluted Adaptor (Step 57) 2.5 µl NEBNext Ligation Enhancer 1 µl NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix 30 µl Total Volume 93.5 µl The Ligation Master Mix and Ligation Enhancer can be mixed ahead of time and is stable for at least 8 hours @ 4°C. We do not recommend premixing the Ligation Master Mix, Ligation Enhancer and adaptor prior to use in the Adaptor Ligation Step. NEBNext Ligation Enhancer New England Biolabs Catalog #E7374 NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7648

steps.title: Set a 100 μl or 200...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μl or 200 μl pipette to 80 μl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. Caution: The NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix is very viscous. Care should be taken to ensure adequate mixing of the ligation reaction, as incomplete mixing will result in reduced ligation efficiency. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance.

steps.title: Incubate 00:15:00 at 20 °C in a thermocycler
steps.instruction: Incubate 00:15:00 at 20 °C in a thermocycler.

steps.title: Add 3 µL (blue) USER Enzyme...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL (blue) USER Enzyme to the ligation mixture from Step 60, resulting in total volume of 96.5 µL NEBNext USER Enzyme New England Biolabs Catalog #E7458

steps.title: Mix well and incubate at 37...
steps.instruction: Mix well and incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 with the heated lid set to ≥ 45 °C

steps.title: Proceed immediately to Purification of the Ligation Reaction
steps.instruction: Proceed immediately to Purification of the Ligation Reaction.

steps.title: [ ! ] Note
steps.instruction: [ ! ] Note: If you are selecting for libraries with larger insert size (> 200 nt) follow the size selection recommendations in Appendix A, Chapter 6. Add 87 µL (0.9X) resuspended SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads and mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. NEBNext Sample Purification Beads New England Biolabs Catalog #E7767

steps.title: Incubate for 00:10:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:10:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Quickly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge and place the tube on an appropriate magnetic rack to separate beads from the supernatant. After the solution is clear (~ 00:05:00 ), discard the supernatant that contains unwanted fragments. Caution: Do not discard beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack. Incubate at room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) Contributed by users

steps.title: Repeat Step 68 once for a...
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 68 once for a total of 2 washing steps. go to step #68

steps.title: Briefly spin the tube, and put...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tube, and put the tube back in the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Completely remove the residual ethanol, and...
steps.instruction: Completely remove the residual ethanol, and air dry beads until the beads are dry for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with the lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack. Elute DNA target from the beads by adding 17 µL 0.1X TE (provided) to the beads. Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down. Quickly spin the tube and incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature. Put the tube in the magnet until the solution is clear.

steps.title: Without disturbing the bead pellet, transfer...
steps.instruction: Without disturbing the bead pellet, transfer 15 µL of the supernatant to a clean PCR tube and proceed to PCR enrichment. If you need to stop at this point in the protocol samples can be stored at –20°C.

[ ! ] Check and verify that the concentration of your oligos is 10 μM on the label. [ ! ] Use Option A for any NEBNext oligos kit where index primers are supplied in tubes. These kits have the forward and reverse primers supplied in separate tubes. Use Option B for any NEBNext oligos kit where index primers are supplied in a 96-well plate format. These kits have the forward and reverse (i7 and i5) primers combined .

steps.title: Set up the PCR reaction as...
steps.instruction: Set up the PCR reaction as described below based on the type of oligos (PCR primers) used. Option A: Forward and Reverse Primers Separate %s %s Component Volume Per One Library Adaptor Ligated DNA (Step 72) 14 µl NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 25 µl Universal PCR Primer/i5 Primer*,** 5 µl Index (X) Primer/i7 Primer*,** 5 µl Total Volume 50 µl Option B: Forward and Reverse Primers Combined %s %s Component Volume Per One Library Adaptor ligated DNA (Step 72) 15 µl NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 25 µl Index (X)/Universal Primer Mix* 10 µl Total Volume 50 µl * NEBNext Oligos must be purchased separately from the library prep kit. Refer to the corresponding NEBNext Oligo kit manual for determining valid barcode combinations. ** Use only one i7 primer/ index primer per sample. Use only one i5 primer (or the universal primer for single index kits) per sample

steps.title: Mix well by gently pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix well by gently pipetting up and down 10 times. Quickly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Place the tube on a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a thermocycler with the heated lid set to 105 °C and perform PCR amplification using the following PCR cycling conditions (refer to Table 77.A and Table 77.B): Table 77.A: %s %s Cycle Step Temp Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 seconds 1 Denaturation 98°C 10 seconds 7–16*,** Annealing/Extension 65°C 75 seconds Final Extension 65°C 5 minutes 1 Hold 4°C ∞ * The number of PCR cycles should be adjusted based on RNA input. ** It is important to limit the number of PCR cycles to avoid overamplification. If overamplification occurs, a second peak ~ 1,000 bp will appear on the Bioanalyzer trace (see Figure 7.2 in manual). Table 77.B: Recommended PCR cycles based on input amount: %s %s Total RNA Input Recommended PCR Cycles 1,000 ng 7–8 100 ng 11–12 10 ng

steps.title: Vortex SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 45 µL (0
steps.instruction: Add 45 µL (0.9X) of resuspended beads to the PCR reaction (~ 50 µL ). Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. NEBNext Sample Purification Beads New England Biolabs Catalog #E6315

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Quickly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge and place the tube on an appropriate magnetic rack to separate beads from the supernatant. After the solution is clear (~ 00:05:00 ), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Caution: Do not discard beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack. Incubate at room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) Contributed by users

steps.title: Repeat Step 82 once for a...
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 82 once for a total of 2 washing steps. go to step #82

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with the lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 23 µL 0.1X TE (provided) to the beads. Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down ten times. Quickly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge and incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature. Place the tube in the magnetic rack until the solution is clear.

steps.title: Transfer 20 µL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 µL of the supernatant to a clean PCR tube, and store at –20°C.

steps.title: Run 1 µL library on a DNA High Sensitivity Chip
steps.instruction: Run 1 µL library on a DNA High Sensitivity Chip. A dilution may be necessary for running on a Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity DNA Chip

steps.title: Check that the electropherogram shows a...
steps.instruction: Check that the electropherogram shows a narrow distribution with a peak size approximately 300 bp. Note: If a peak at ~ 80 bp (primers) or 128 bp (adaptor-dimer) is visible in the bioanalyzer traces, bring up the sample volume (from Step 86) to 50 μl with 0.1X TE buffer and repeat the SPRIselect Bead or NEBNext Sample Purification Bead Cleanup Step (Section "Purification of the PCR Reaction using SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads").

steps.title: Figure 89
steps.instruction: Figure 89: Example of RNA library size distribution on a Bioanalyzer.

steps.title: Briefly spin down the samples in a microcentrifuge
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the samples in a microcentrifuge.
